title: Sichuan Chicken Chili

ingredients.title: For the Chicken:
items: 250g boneless chicken thigh (preferably skin-on), cut into 1.5 cm cubes
1/2 egg white (or 1 whole small egg white)
Neutral oil for deep-frying (about 300ml, depending on pan size)

ingredients.title: Aromatics & Chilies:
items: 1 cup dried Chinese red chilies, deseeded and halved
Optional: 1 tbsp roasted peanuts or toasted sesame seeds

ingredients.title: Optional Seasoning (Final Toss):
items: Optional: few drops of sesame oil

instruction.title: Marinate the Chicken:
steps: In a bowl, combine chicken with Shaoxing wine, soy sauce, salt, pepper, egg white, and cornstarch.
Mix until evenly coated. Let sit for 20 minutes.

instruction.title: Fry the Chicken:
steps: Heat oil to 180°C (350°F) in a wok or saucepan.
Fry the chicken in small batches for 2–3 minutes, until golden and just crisp.
Drain on paper towels or a wire rack.

instruction.title: Prepare Final Stir-Fry:
steps: Pour out excess oil, keeping 2 tbsp in the wok.
On medium heat, add Sichuan peppercorns and fry for 15 seconds until fragrant.
Add dried chilies, stir-fry until slightly puffed but not burned (20–30 seconds).
Add ginger, garlic, and scallion. Stir until aromatic.
Add the fried chicken and stir-fry over high heat for 1–2 minutes to combine flavors.
Taste and adjust salt. Optionally add a pinch of sugar and few drops sesame oil.

chefTips: Serve immediately on a hot plate.
Not all chilies are meant to be eaten — traditionally, you pick the chicken out from the chili bed.
Best with steamed white rice or Sichuan-style cold noodles.
